facts: Saturn's moon Titan is the ONLY moon in our Solar System with a dense atmosphere – it's actually thicker than Earth's!
It's also the ONLY body besides Earth with stable liquid on its surface: vast lakes, rivers, and seas.
But these aren't water! They're made of liquid METHANE and ETHANE.
At a frigid -179°C (-290°F), Titan has a complete 'methane cycle' with clouds, precipitation, and flowing hydrocarbons!
idea.payoff: Imagine a literally alien world with its own flammable weather system! 🤯
idea.title: Saturn's Moon Rains Fuel! ⛽🤯

scenes.visual: Stylized rain clouds transforming into a fuel pump icon, with a subtle transition effect.

scenes.text: Titan: ONLY moon with a dense atmosphere. Thicker than Earth's!
scenes.visual: Animated representation of Titan orbiting Saturn, with its thick, hazy atmospheric layers visibly highlighted and swirling.

scenes.text: ONLY other body with liquid surface: Lakes, Rivers, Seas!
scenes.visual: Aerial view of Titan's surface, showing vast dark liquid bodies resembling terrestrial lakes, rivers, and coastlines under an orange sky.

scenes.text: NOT WATER! It's liquid METHANE & ETHANE. (-179°C) Complete 'Methane Cycle'!
scenes.visual: Infographic animation illustrating a methane cycle: clouds forming, 'rain' falling into a lake, evaporation, with a temperature overlay of -179°C.
Dramatic rendering of a stormy Titan surface, with lightning flashes illuminating the methane clouds and liquid surface, implying a volatile atmosphere.
